meta.title: TB-CDA-5: Flexible Modeling, Interactive Dashboards, and Efficient Analytics

questions.stem: A Tableau author needs a local snapshot of source data that can improve performance and support offline work. Which connection mode should be considered first?

options.explanation: Extracts create a local snapshot of data that can improve performance and support offline analysis. Live connections query the source directly.

questions.stem: Two tables from the same source need to be combined row by row because they share identical columns. Which operation should be used on the data source page?

options.explanation: A union appends rows from tables with the same structure. Relationships and sets serve different purposes.

questions.stem: A Tableau author wants to create a user-controlled input that swaps measures in a calculation. Which feature is designed for this?

options.explanation: Parameters provide user-controlled values that can drive calculations, reference lines, and other interactive behaviors.

questions.stem: Which Tableau feature lets you plot several measures in one view without creating a separate worksheet for each measure?

options.explanation: Measure Names and Measure Values let you show multiple measures in one visualization efficiently.

questions.stem: A dashboard should filter one worksheet when the user clicks marks in another worksheet. Which dashboard capability should be configured?

options.explanation: Filter actions pass selected marks to other sheets or dashboards to update the view interactively.

questions.stem: A field stores values like `$1,245.75` as text. What should the author do before performing accurate numeric aggregation?

options.text: Clean the string and convert it to a numeric data type
options.explanation: Tableau calculations depend on appropriate data types. Currency symbols stored as text should be cleaned and cast to a numeric type.

options.text: Put the field on Color to infer currency

questions.stem: A business user asks for customer-level totals that ignore the view’s month granularity. Which calculation type is most appropriate?

options.explanation: FIXED LOD expressions compute results at a specified dimensional level independent of the current visualization granularity.

questions.stem: A worksheet needs a running total across months. Which Tableau feature directly supports that requirement?

options.explanation: Running totals are classic table calculation use cases in Tableau.

questions.stem: A data source contains a category field with several values that should be grouped into a single “Other” member for analysis. Which feature should be used?

options.explanation: Groups combine dimension members into higher-level categories for simpler analysis.

questions.stem: A team wants to display sales and profit on the same chart, each with its own axis, and optionally synchronize scales when appropriate. Which chart technique is most relevant?

options.explanation: Dual-axis charts are used to plot multiple measures with separate axes and can be synchronized when useful.

questions.stem: Which design choices usually improve Tableau dashboard performance? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Reducing mark count and using extracts appropriately are standard Tableau performance practices. Extra floating objects and excessive actions can add overhead.

options.text: Use extracts when they fit the workload
options.explanation: Reducing mark count and using extracts appropriately are standard Tableau performance practices. Extra floating objects and excessive actions can add overhead.

options.text: Add more floating objects just for decoration
Create a separate dashboard action for every mark

questions.stem: A workbook combines tables with logical relationships instead of physically joining every table into one denormalized table. What is a key benefit of relationships?

options.text: They preserve table-level detail and can reduce unnecessary row duplication
options.explanation: Relationships keep tables separate at the logical layer and can avoid row duplication problems that arise from aggressive physical joins.

options.text: They work only with map layers

questions.stem: A continuous numeric field is needed for a histogram with buckets such as 0–9, 10–19, and 20–29. Which feature should be created?

options.explanation: Bins group continuous numeric values into discrete ranges for histogram-style analysis.

questions.stem: A worksheet should compute percent of total within each region independently, restarting the calculation for each region. What must the author set correctly?

options.explanation: Table calculations depend on compute-using and partitioning settings to determine where the calculation restarts.

questions.stem: A team wants a guided sequence of views with captions that tell a narrative to executives. Which Tableau feature best matches this need?

options.explanation: Story points create sequenced, captioned views intended for presentations and narrative analytics.

questions.stem: When should a live connection often be preferred over an extract?

options.text: When near-real-time access to source changes matters more than snapshot performance
options.explanation: Live connections query the source directly and are commonly chosen when users need current source data.

options.text: When the workbook must always work offline

questions.stem: Which tasks are common uses of parameters in Tableau? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Parameters often drive calculations, swaps, and reference lines. They do not independently implement row-level security or change raw field types.

options.text: Control reference lines or user-selected logic
options.explanation: Parameters often drive calculations, swaps, and reference lines. They do not independently implement row-level security or change raw field types.

questions.stem: A map shows many overlapping points, making density hard to interpret. Which visualization approach can help summarize concentration?

options.explanation: Density maps help show concentration patterns for large sets of marks in geographic views.

questions.stem: A team uses two tables from different databases and wants to combine related fields in one worksheet. Which Tableau capability supports this scenario?

options.text: Cross-database joins or relationships depending the model design
options.explanation: Tableau supports combining data across connections through cross-database modeling options, including joins and relationships.

questions.stem: A published workbook should restrict visible rows based on the signed-in viewer. Which Tableau feature is most directly aligned with that requirement?

options.explanation: User filters are a built-in way to personalize row visibility based on the authenticated user or group.

questions.stem: A worksheet should let users compare actual values to a benchmark line such as average sales. Which Tableau capability is appropriate?

options.text: Analytics pane reference or average lines
options.explanation: The Analytics pane provides reference and average lines for comparative analysis in a view.

questions.stem: What is a major difference between a FIXED LOD expression and a regular table calculation?

options.text: FIXED LOD computes at a defined dimensional level independent of the final table-calc addressing
options.explanation: FIXED LOD expressions define a granularity in the calculation itself, while table calculations operate on marks already present in the visualization.

options.text: FIXED LOD can only be used on maps
Table calculations always ignore the view

questions.stem: Which tasks are typically part of organizing and simplifying data in Tableau? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Groups and sets are core data organization features. Story points and dashboard actions are presentation-layer features.
Groups and sets are core data organization features. Story points and dashboard actions are presentation-layer features.

questions.stem: A team wants Tableau to identify natural segments in a scatter plot without manually defining rules. Which feature should they try?

options.explanation: Tableau clustering can automatically identify groups of marks using statistical segmentation.

questions.stem: A dashboard layout must adapt to phone and tablet screens with alternate arrangements of objects. Which capability addresses this?

options.explanation: Device-specific layouts let authors optimize dashboard arrangements for different screen types.

questions.stem: A field should display custom labels such as changing `CA` to `California` without changing the underlying data values. Which feature is most appropriate?

options.explanation: Aliases change the displayed member labels while preserving the underlying data values.

questions.stem: Which statements about dual-axis views are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: They can display two measures in one chart
options.explanation: Dual-axis views are used for multiple measures and can synchronize axes when it makes analytical sense. They are not limited to maps or discrete measures.

options.text: Axes can be synchronized when the measures are comparable
options.explanation: Dual-axis views are used for multiple measures and can synchronize axes when it makes analytical sense. They are not limited to maps or discrete measures.

options.text: They are only available for map views

questions.stem: What is the purpose of assigning a geographic role to a field such as State or Country?

options.text: To help Tableau interpret the field for map generation
options.explanation: Geographic roles tell Tableau how to interpret location data for mapping.

options.text: To convert the field into a table calculation
To publish a workbook to Server

questions.stem: A dashboard should let users click a mark and jump to another sheet that shows more detail for that selection. Which action type is best?

options.text: A filter action or go-to-sheet style dashboard interaction
options.explanation: Dashboard actions support selection-driven navigation and filtering between views.

questions.stem: A team wants to let users choose between Sales, Profit, and Quantity in the same chart with a single control. Which design is common?

options.explanation: Parameters combined with calculations are a common pattern for measure switching in Tableau.

options.text: A user filter on workbook access

questions.stem: A worksheet needs to show month-over-month percent change. Which feature is a natural fit?

options.text: A table calculation such as percent difference
options.explanation: Month-over-month percent change is commonly implemented with table calculations over ordered date marks.

options.text: A set that groups all months

questions.stem: Which practices help create maintainable dashboards? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use layout containers to organize objects
options.explanation: Layout containers improve structure, and reducing clutter improves usability and maintainability. Unstructured floating layouts tend to be harder to maintain.
Layout containers improve structure, and reducing clutter improves usability and maintainability. Unstructured floating layouts tend to be harder to maintain.

options.text: Create as many floating sheets as possible without structure
Hide filters that users need constantly

questions.stem: A worksheet should exclude irrelevant rows before the view renders, reducing what the user sees and what Tableau processes. Which feature is most directly used?

options.explanation: Filters narrow the rows contributing to the view and are a core mechanism for reducing the data shown.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of a set in Tableau?

options.text: To define a subset of data members for comparison or focus
options.explanation: Sets let authors define subsets of members for analysis and comparison, either manually or based on conditions.

options.text: To change a measure from continuous to discrete automatically

questions.stem: A business user wants to add a forecast line to a time-series chart. Which Tableau area contains this capability?

options.explanation: Forecasting and similar analytical features are added from the Analytics pane for supported visualizations.

questions.stem: What is a primary reason to use a dashboard instead of a single worksheet?

options.text: To combine multiple views and controls into one analytical canvas
options.explanation: Dashboards assemble multiple sheets and interactive objects into a single user experience.

options.text: To replace all calculations with aliases

questions.stem: A Tableau author is deciding between a physical join and a relationship. Which considerations favor relationships? (Select TWO)

options.text: Avoiding unnecessary row multiplication across tables
options.explanation: Relationships often help preserve separate levels of detail and reduce unintended row multiplication compared with aggressive physical joins.

options.text: Preserving each table’s level of detail until query time
options.explanation: Relationships often help preserve separate levels of detail and reduce unintended row multiplication compared with aggressive physical joins.

options.text: Forcing all tables into one denormalized extract always

questions.stem: A map contains locations Tableau does not recognize automatically. What can the author do?

options.text: Provide custom geocoding or correct geographic roles
options.explanation: Custom geocoding and correct geographic roles help Tableau interpret unrecognized locations.

options.text: Convert the field to a story point

questions.stem: A calculated field should return different labels depending on product category. Which expression style is most appropriate?

options.explanation: Conditional logic in calculated fields is the standard way to derive labels from category values.

questions.stem: Executives say a dashboard feels slow when opening and interacting. Which review should the author prioritize first?

options.text: A performance-focused review of mark count, query load, and extract/live design
options.explanation: Performance tuning in Tableau usually starts with workload drivers such as mark count, query behavior, and connection design rather than cosmetic changes.
